mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts household income and consumption demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in industrial production and a decline in housing starts indicates supply-side inflationary pressure without corresponding demand support, forcing margin compression and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in at least 50% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term loses statistical significance when added to a single-factor INDPRO model.

mechanism_narrative: Stabilization of the consumer price index at current levels confirms that inflation expectations are anchored, allowing equity valuations to expand and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL stabilizes but SPX forward 60-day returns remain flat or negative, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is held constant.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real personal savings exceeding 5 percent signals household deleveraging and consumption pullback, which reduces corporate revenue growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no incremental explanatory power over PAYEMS growth.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and aggregate supply growth, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-month correlation between INDPRO changes and SPX 20-day returns remains below 0.10, or forward returns do not differ significantly between rising and falling INDPRO periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample testing, or SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of confirmed labor expansion episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in real GDP growth below 2.0 percent annualized rate indicates weakening aggregate demand, which compresses equity risk premiums and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive when GDPC1 growth decelerates below 2.0%, or the signal exhibits no statistical edge after controlling for FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL.
